domanda: 1. In the past, Wide Area Networks (WAN):
opzioneA: Were intended as geographic networks designed mainly for low bitrate applications
opzioneB: Were intended as shared networks designed mainly to exchange large amount of data
opzioneC: Were intended as geographic networks designed mainly to exchange large amount of data
opzioneD: Were intended as campus networks designed mainly to connect several buildings to each other
motivazione: WANs originally relied on low-bandwidth telecom links spread over large geographical areas.

domanda: 2. In the past, Local Area Networks (LAN):
opzioneA: Were intended as local networks designed mainly for low bitrate applications
opzioneB: Were intended as shared networks designed mainly to exchange large amount of data
opzioneC: Were intended as shared networks designed mainly for "bursty" application
opzioneD: Were intended as campus networks designed mainly to connect several buildings to each other
motivazione: LANs were designed for local, high-speed, bursty traffic sharing a common medium.

domanda: 3. In networks based on a shared communication medium:
opzioneA: While a station is transmitting, other stations cannot transmit
opzioneB: While a station is transmitting, other stations can transmit only if the previous communica- tion is not directed to them
opzioneC: While a station is transmitting, other stations can receive and transmit at the same time
opzioneD: All stations can transmit at any time without any risk of collision thanks to the presence of the switch
motivazione: In a shared medium (like a hub), collisions occur if multiple nodes transmit simultaneously, meaning only one can transmit at a time.

domanda: 4. In a LAN:
opzioneA: There is always a intermediate device that handles the frames
opzioneB: All devices have the same priority in getting access to the shared communication medium
opzioneC: All devices have the same priority in getting access to the shared communication medium, except a possible intermediate device that is privileged against other stations
opzioneD: If needed, we can configure a possible device (e.g. server) that has higher priority in getting access to the shared communication medium
motivazione: Early LANs treated all nodes equally when accessing the shared medium, without inherent prioritization.

domanda: 5. A shared communication medium technology is characterized by:
opzioneA: Broadcast communication, no intermediate sistems and high flexibility
opzioneB: Low cost, thanks to the deployment of level 2 communication devices (L2 Switch)
opzioneC: Reliability, privacy reasonably guaranteed for users, but impossibility to have multiple communications at the same time between hosts
opzioneD: Reliability, possibility to have multiple communications at the same time between hosts, but degree of privacy
motivazione: Shared mediums inherently broadcast signals to all connected nodes and historically lacked intermediate systems.

domanda: 6. The OSI level named "Data Link":
opzioneA: Assumes that physical-layer functions are defined, which are the ones used to allow several networks technologies (e.g. Ethernet, WiFI) to communicate together
opzioneB: Can implements multicast/broadcast addresses that can be exploited for Solicitation and Advertisement functions
opzioneC: Defines how to generate bits over the channel
opzioneD: Can be divided into a LCC (Link Central Control) sublevel and a MAC (Medium Access Control) sublevel
motivazione: The Data Link layer uses MAC addressing, which includes broadcast and multicast addresses utilized by higher layer protocols (like ARP).

domanda: 7. The OSI level named "Physical":
opzioneA: It is specified only for Ethernet networks
opzioneB: It is the same in all cabled networks (e.g. Ethernet, Token Ring, FDDI), and basically is derived from the specifications of the original Ethernet
opzioneC: It can be shared among several network technologies, witch however differ on Data-Link level (e.g., Ethernet and FDDI)
opzioneD: It defines the way to get access to the shared communication medium
motivazione: The physical layer defines bit encoding and transmission, and can be common across differing Data Link implementations.

domanda: 8. The LLC (Logical Link Control) sublevel:
opzioneA: It has almost disappeared from modern networks
opzioneB: It was never able to succeed on WiFi networks, which use another framing format instead
opzioneC: It is present in a few protocols operating on Ethernet networks
opzioneD: It defines 64 bit addresses to identify the level 3 protocol contained in the frame
motivazione: LLC is mostly legacy but is still used for specific protocols (like Spanning Tree) to identify upper-layer payloads.

domanda: 9. The MAC (Medium Access Control) sublevel:
opzioneA: It defines the arbitration mechanism in order to get access to the shared communication medium
opzioneB: It defines a field named "Protocol Type" that identifies the level 3 protocol contained in the frame
opzioneC: It is present in all LAN technologies, except in Ethernet where is usually replaced by LLC-SNAP
opzioneD: It defines how bits must transmitted over the physical channel
motivazione: MAC defines the arbitration rules (like CSMA/CD) to manage and resolve access to the shared physical medium.

domanda: 10. MAC addresses:
opzioneA: Are allocated by government authorities
opzioneB: Are set by the user (or operating system) when the devices is used for the first time
opzioneC: Are set by the manufacturer: the first 3 bytes identify the vendor and remaining are defined by an algorithm that generates random 24 bits numbers
opzioneD: Are set by the manufacturer in each network interface card
motivazione: MAC addresses are physically burned-in by vendors (3 bytes for the OUI, 3 bytes specific to the NIC).

domanda: 11. If the operating system of a station receives all the packets that are in fact received by the network interface card:
opzioneA: The station does not have an IP address
opzioneB: The station is connected to a switch
opzioneC: The station has a multicast MAC address
opzioneD: The network card is in "promiscuous" mode
motivazione: Promiscuous mode forces the NIC to pass all received traffic to the OS, bypassing the hardware MAC filter.

domanda: 12. The difference between "frame" and "packet" is:
opzioneA: A frame identifies level 2 data, whereas a packet identify level 3 data
opzioneB: A frame identifies level 3 data, whereas a packet identify level 2 data
opzioneC: They are synonymous
opzioneD: A frame identifies Ethernet data, whereas a packet identify other protocols data
motivazione: Frames are Layer 2 Protocol Data Units (PDUs), while packets are Layer 3 PDUs.

domanda: 13. If a host is connected for the first time to a shared Ethernet LAN and the user types the PING command toward an other station:
opzioneA: It generates immediately and ICMP Echo Request packet toward the destination, since we are on a shared communication medium
opzioneB: It generates first an ARP packet in order to know the destination MAC address of the destination host, that will reply with its IP address
opzioneC: If the destination host belongs to a different level 3 network, it must generate a packet that discovers the MAC address of its default gateway
opzioneD: It generates an ARP Request, that has the side effect of updating the filtering database of all stations
motivazione: ARP is required to find the MAC of the default gateway when the ultimate IP destination resides on a different subnet.

domanda: 14. A host "A" is turned on and connected for the first time to a shared Ethernet LAN. Then, the user types the PING command toward the server "S" "www.polito.it", which is connected to the same LAN. The first frame that will be generated on the network will be:
opzioneA: An ARP Request toward the DNS if "S" belongs to a different level 3 network
opzioneB: A DNS Query toward the DNS if "A" belongs to the same IP network of the DNS and the router
opzioneC: An ARP Request containing the router IP address in the "Target IP Address" field if "S" belongs to a different IP network compared to the host
opzioneD: An ARP Request containing the router IP address in the "Target IP Address" field if the DNS server belongs to a different IP network compared to the host
motivazione: To resolve the URL, a DNS query is needed. If the DNS server is off-subnet, the host must first ARP for its default gateway.

domanda: 15. A frame that contains the MAC address of a router as destination address:
opzioneA: It will have the IP address of the router as destination IP address
opzioneB: It will have the IP address of a station does not belong to local LAN as destination IP address
opzioneC: It will have the IP address of a station that belongs to local LAN as destination IP address
opzioneD: It will have the IP address of the router or the one of a station that does not belong to the same IP network of the source host as destination IP address
motivazione: The Layer 2 destination is the router's MAC, but the Layer 3 destination is the final remote IP (or the router's IP if communicating with it directly).

domanda: 16. Before an ICMP Echo Request packet, the trasmitting station:
opzioneA: It always generates an ARP Request
opzioneB: It always generates a DNS Query
opzioneC: It always generates an ARP Request and a DNS Query
opzioneD: It does not necessarily generate additional packets
motivazione: ARP and DNS resolutions might already be cached by the OS, so they are not always strictly generated.

domanda: 17. Main characteristics of a physical copper link, with respect to data transmission are:
opzioneA: Propagation speed (expressed as a fraction of the light speed), impedance of the line (expressed in AWG, American Wire Gage), size of the physical copper wire
opzioneB: Impedance of the line (expressed in AWG, American Wire Gage), size of the physical copper wire
opzioneC: Propagation speed (expressed as a fraction of the light speed), impedance of the line, size of the physical copper wire (expressed in AWG, American Wire Gage)
opzioneD: Propagation speed (expressed as a fraction of the light speed), impedance of the line, link length (expressed in AWG, American Wire Gage)
motivazione: Copper links are physically defined by their propagation speed, electrical impedance, and physical wire diameter (AWG).

domanda: 18. The AWG (American Wire Gage):
opzioneA: Measures the signal attenuation
opzioneB: Indicates the maximum noise tolerated by the cable
opzioneC: Measures the size of the physical copper wire
opzioneD: Measures the size of the core of a fiber optic link
motivazione: AWG is the standardized measurement system for the diameter of physical copper wires.

domanda: 19. The Cross-Talk:
opzioneA: Is called "Alien" if it generated by a generic external source of noise
opzioneB: Is due to attenuation of a signal into a copper wire
opzioneC: Is due to interference generated by the other physical links in the cable
opzioneD: It can be present if a plug is installed in the wrong way or if the wire has several curves on its path
motivazione: Cross-Talk is the electromagnetic interference leaking from one adjacent wire to another within the same cable.

domanda: 20. With respect to fiber optic cables:
opzioneA: Multimode fibers with led technology are the most recent and have the highest bandwidth
opzioneB: Multimode fibers have low dispersion rate and high bandwidth
opzioneC: Indoor cables do not need an external metallic shield
opzioneD: Singlemode fibers with laser technology are the most recent and have the highest bandwidth
motivazione: Single-mode fiber uses highly focused lasers, providing the longest distances and highest potential bandwidths.

domanda: 21. In structured cabling:
opzioneA: The structure usually follows a tree, in which several portions are aggregated together at different levels
opzioneB: It is a good rule to locate the Floor Distribution and the Building Distributor in the same room, so they can be connected to each other using short 10Gb cables
opzioneC: Usually a ring is preferred to a tree topology because of its robustness
opzioneD: Copper wires can span up to 100m, plus the additional patch cords at their ends (e.g. user station and network device)
motivazione: Structured cabling uses a hierarchical tree design to manage complexity and scale out network drops efficiently.

domanda: 22. Copper wires in a data-center:
opzioneA: Are not used due to very high high communication speed requested
opzioneB: Are heavily used throughout the entire data-center mainly for budget reasons
opzioneC: Are used in some portions of the data-center (where cables are reasonably short), mainly for budget reasons
opzioneD: Are usually used to connect disks, which are characterized by lower speed than servers
motivazione: Copper is highly cost-effective for short distances, making it popular for Top-of-Rack connections in data centers.

domanda: 23. When transmitting on an Ethernet network, a station sends a "jamming sequence" if:
opzioneA: The current trasmission was succesfull
opzioneB: It wants to "reinforce" a collision
opzioneC: It wants to take the ownneship of the channel
opzioneD: It wants to release the channel
motivazione: A jamming sequence ensures that all stations on the collision domain can clearly detect that a collision has occurred.

domanda: 24. After a collision on an Ethernet network:
opzioneA: All the stations can immediately compete for the ownership of the physical channel for a new transmission
opzioneB: The stations that generate the collision (and only these ones) can compete for the ownership of the physical channel
opzioneC: Stations that generated the collision must wait a random time, called "back-off", before competing again for the ownership of the physical channel
opzioneD: Only the station that generated collision (i.e., the station that started transmit later) must wait a random time, called "back-off", before competing again for the ownership of the physical channel
motivazione: The CSMA/CD protocol requires involved stations to wait a random back-off timer before attempting transmission again.

domanda: 25. In order to detect collisions on an Ethernet network, we need to consider:
opzioneA: Distance among stations, minimum frame size, signal propagation speed
opzioneB: Distance among stations, link bandwidth, minimum frame size
opzioneC: Distance among stations, link bandwidth, minimum frame size, signal propagation speed
opzioneD: Number of stations on the network, collision detection speed, minimum duration of the transmission
motivazione: Collision detection relies on the frame transmission lasting longer than the maximum round-trip propagation delay of the network.

domanda: 26. To distinguish an Ethernet 2.0 (DIX) frame from an IEEE 802.3 frame:
opzioneA: We can check the "Version" field at the beginning of frame
opzioneB: We can check the value of the 2 bytes corresponding to the "Ethertype" field in Ethernet 2.0 (or "Length" field in IEEE 802.3)
opzioneC: We can calculate the size of the data carried in the frame; in case this value is is lower than 64 bytes, the frame is IEEE 802.3, otherwise it is Ethernet 2.0
opzioneD: We can look at the possible presence of an LLC SNAP envelope, that is only present in the IEE 802.3 frame
motivazione: A value > 1536 indicates an EtherType (Ethernet II format), while a value <= 1500 indicates payload Length (802.3 format).

domanda: 27. Frames Ethernet 2.0 (DIX) and IEEE 802.3:
opzioneA: Are not compatible and have different sizes for the minimum frame
opzioneB: Are compatible, even if they have different sizes for the minimum frame
opzioneC: Are compatible, even if they have different sizes for the maximum frame
opzioneD: Are compatible, even if only the second can contain the "Padding" field
motivazione: They can coexist on the same wire, though 802.3 specifically defines a padding field for undersized frames.

domanda: 28. In Ethernet, the Inter-frame gap is:
opzioneA: The required silence between a frame and the following
opzioneB: Empty data within the frame, which allow reaching the minimum frame size needed to detect a collision
opzioneC: The set of bytes sent on the channel acting as end frame delimiter
opzioneD: The time needed to send the bytes of the preamble (up to 7 bytes maximum)
motivazione: The Inter-frame gap is a mandatory 96-bit idle period between frames, allowing network receivers to recover and process.

domanda: 29. The maximum collision diameter in an Ethernet network:
opzioneA: Can be seen as the maximum length of a link between a host and a hub
opzioneB: Can be seen as the maximum length of a link between a host and a bridge
opzioneC: Does not change even if hubs are replaced by bridges in the network
opzioneD: It is approximately 200m in case of copper cables
motivazione: Bridges and switches segment collision domains, so the maximum collision diameter is restricted between the host and the bridge.

domanda: 30. What does it happen to a PC with its network interface in Full Duplex Fixed mode if it is connected to a switch with its network interface in auto-negotiation mode?
opzioneA: The interface of the switch goes in half duplex mode and it may detect some fake collisions
opzioneB: The interface of the switch goes automatically into Full Duplex Mode
opzioneC: PC and switch will never be able to communicate
opzioneD: PC and switch will communicate without problems
motivazione: If auto-negotiation fails, the switch defaults to half-duplex, resulting in a duplex mismatch and collisions.

domanda: 31. The handshake of the transmission speed among two Ethernet interfaces takes place:
opzioneA: At layer 1
opzioneB: At layer 2
opzioneC: At layers 1 & 2
opzioneD: Partially at layer 1 and partially at layer 2
motivazione: Auto-negotiation operates purely at the physical layer using Fast Link Pulses.

domanda: 32. In an Ethernet network based entirely on switches with all links in Full Duplex Mode, how many collisions we may experience?
opzioneA: It depends on the size of the network
opzioneB: It depends on the number of the switches
opzioneC: It depends on the number of connected PCs
opzioneD: We do not have collisions
motivazione: Full-duplex micro-segmentation completely disables CSMA/CD, eliminating the possibility of collisions.

domanda: 33. The loss of connectivity between two L2 entities (e.g. host or switch), directly connected, can be immediately detected at the physical layer if the link is in:
opzioneA: Full duplex
opzioneB: Half duplex
opzioneC: Full duplex, with the other side of the link connected to a switch
opzioneD: Generally it cannot be detected
motivazione: Full-duplex links constantly exchange idle signals on dedicated transmit/receive pairs, detecting physical link drops instantly.

domanda: 34. A broken network cable can be immediately detected at the physical layer if the link is in:
opzioneA: Full duplex
opzioneB: Half duplex
opzioneC: Full duplex, with the other side of the link connected to a switch
opzioneD: Generally, a broken network cable will be immediately detected by the entities directly connected to the two edges of the cable itself
motivazione: Point-to-point electrical links instantly detect a loss of carrier signal at the edges of the connection.

domanda: 35. The network is defined as "switched" when:
opzioneA: It contains only switches and end stations
opzioneB: It contains switches, hubs and end stations
opzioneC: It contains at least one router
opzioneD: It is based on a shared physical medium for communication
motivazione: A purely switched network eliminates hubs, replacing all shared collision domains with dedicated switchports.

domanda: 36. In a switched network:
opzioneA: Loosing frames is a rare event thanks to the high speed of the switch
opzioneB: There are no lost frames thanks to the fact that there are no collisions
opzioneC: The number of lost frames may not be negligible because of congestions on the switches
opzioneD: There are no lost frames thanks to the use of Spanning Tree protocol
motivazione: Although collisions are gone, switch port buffers can overflow during heavy microbursts or congestion, causing dropped frames.

domanda: 37. Switches:
opzioneA: Usually have a single MAC address for the entire device
opzioneB: Have one MAC address per physical port
opzioneC: Do not have any MAC address (they are "transparent" indeed)
opzioneD: Have one MAC address per physical port plus one for each configured VLAN
motivazione: Switches have a base system MAC address used for management traffic and Spanning Tree BPDUs.

domanda: 38. One of the reasons that motivates a network designer to use VLANs is:
opzioneA: Faster frame forwarding
opzioneB: Broadcast traffic reduction
opzioneC: Simplified management of Spanning Tree
opzioneD: Collision reduction
motivazione: VLANs logically segment the network, isolating and shrinking broadcast domains to improve efficiency.

domanda: 39. Two PCs belonging to two different VLANs:
opzioneA: Can communicate normally by sending Ethernet frames directly to the other party
opzioneB: Can never send Ethernet frames directly to the other party
opzioneC: One of the two PCs must use a network interface with VLAN IEEE 802.1q tags support
opzioneD: Both PC must use a network interface with VLAN IEEE 802.1q tags support
motivazione: Due to Layer 2 isolation, inter-VLAN communication strictly requires a Layer 3 routing device.

domanda: 40. Two PCs directly connected to the same Hub (repeater):
opzioneA: Cannot belong to the different VLAN
opzioneB: Will always belong to the same VLAN
opzioneC: Can belong to different VLANs if they use a network interface compatible with the IEEE 802.1q standard
opzioneD: Can belong to different VLANs if they use a network interface compatible with the IEEE 802.1q standard, but there may be some delivery problem with respect to large packets
motivazione: Endpoints can process 802.1Q tags directly, but hubs forward the frames verbatim, which can cause MTU issues if the frames exceed standard lengths.

domanda: 41. Two stations configured in "trunk" mode and belonging to the same VLAN are connected to the same switch that does not support the IEEE 802.1q (VLAN) standard:
opzioneA: The stations cannot exchange data unless a router is being used
opzioneB: The stations cannot exchange data even if a router is being used
opzioneC: The stations can always exchange data even if a router is not being used
opzioneD: The stations can always exchange data even if a router is not being used, but there may be some delivery problem with respect to large packets
motivazione: An unaware switch simply treats 802.1Q tagged frames as normal payload data, provided the increased frame size doesn't exceed its MTU.

domanda: 42. According to the IEEE 802.1q (VLAN) standard, the connection between two switches:
opzioneA: It can be only of type "Trunk"
opzioneB: It must never be of type "Trunk"
opzioneC: It could be of type "Access"
opzioneD: It could be of type "Access" only if the corresponding ports on the two switches are part of the same VLANS
motivazione: A connection between switches can be an Access link if it only needs to bridge a single, untagged VLAN.

domanda: 43. According to the IEEE 802.1q, the GVRP protocol (GARP VLAN Registration Protocol) is useful to:
opzioneA: Propagate automatically over the whole network the information about the existing VLANS
opzioneB: Assign to packets belonging to different VLANs different priorities
opzioneC: Configure dynamically the ports of a switch based on the configuration obtained from the network interface of the PC at the other side of the cable
opzioneD: Register the traffic of different VLANs
motivazione: GVRP allows switches to dynamically register and share VLAN configuration information across the bridging domain.

domanda: 44. According to the IEEE 802.1q standard, VLANs are configured on the switch:
opzioneA: On each port through an appropriate operation
opzioneB: By the manufacture and it is not possible to modify its configuration
opzioneC: By the manufacture and it is possible to change VLANs just for a restricted number of ports
opzioneD: Only on Trunk ports
motivazione: VLAN membership is fundamentally a port-based configuration applied by the administrator.

domanda: 45. According to the IEEE 802.1q, the membership of a PC to a specific VLAN can be derived from:
opzioneA: The MAC address of the network interface of the PC, independently from the configuration of the switch
opzioneB: The IP address of the network interface of the PC, independently from the switch configura- tion
opzioneC: The MAC and IP addresses of the network interface of the PC, independently from the switch configuration
opzioneD: The configuration of the port on the switch connected to the PC
motivazione: Standard VLAN membership is entirely dictated by the access VLAN assigned to the switchport.

domanda: 46. A port is of type "Trunk" when:
opzioneA: It transmits only frames that do not include the IEEE 802.1q tag
opzioneB: It transmits only BPDU frames
opzioneC: It transmits only frames that include the IEEE 802.1q tag
opzioneD: It transmits only BPDU frames that includes the IEEE 802.1q tag
motivazione: Trunk ports use 802.1Q tags to multiplex and carry frames for multiple VLANs over a single link.

domanda: 47. When Broadcast Storm occurs within a VLAN:
opzioneA: The other VLANs are protected and do not perceives in any way this problem
opzioneB: The broadcast storm occurs also in the other VLANs
opzioneC: The broadcast storm occurs also in the other VLANs, unless a priority mechanism is being used to privilege the traffic of those VLANs
opzioneD: The other VLAN may suffer from delays because of the traffic on the trunk links
motivazione: The broadcast storm is logically contained to its own VLAN, but it can saturate the bandwidth of shared physical trunk links.

domanda: 48. The Spanning Tree protocol is used to:
opzioneA: Transform a network containing meshes into a tree, canceling loops
opzioneB: Manage multiple paths in load-balancing, providing redundancy in the network
opzioneC: Optimize the forwarding process
opzioneD: Update the filtering database
motivazione: STP logically blocks redundant paths to prevent Layer 2 loops, transforming a meshed topology into a loop-free tree.

domanda: 49. The Spanning Tree protocol operates sequentially according to the following phases:
opzioneA: Root Ports selection, Root Bridge selection, Designated Ports selection
opzioneB: Root Bridge selection, Designated Ports selection, Root Ports selection
opzioneC: Root Bridge selection, Root Ports selection, Designated Ports selection
opzioneD: Root Ports selection, Designated Ports selection, Root Bridge selection
motivazione: STP converges by first electing the Root Bridge, then identifying Root Ports on non-root bridges, and finally electing Designated Ports for segments.

domanda: 50. The Spanning Tree Protocol can have two types of Bridge Protocol Data Unit (BPDU) frames:
opzioneA: Configuration and Topology Change Notification
opzioneB: Configuration and Topology Advertisement
opzioneC: Topology Change Notification and Topology Advertisement
opzioneD: Root Bridge Election and Topology Advertisement
motivazione: Configuration BPDUs build and maintain the tree, while TCN BPDUs trigger MAC table flushes during topology changes.

domanda: 51. During the selection of the Root Bridge, the Spanning Tree process operates by analyzing the value of the following field present in the BPDU:
opzioneA: Root Identifier
opzioneB: Root Path Cost
opzioneC: Port Identifier
opzioneD: Bridge Priority
motivazione: The Root Identifier field combines Bridge Priority and MAC address; the switch with the lowest value wins.

domanda: 52. According to the standard, the tree obtained by the Spanning Tree Protocol is computed:
opzioneA: Starting from the physical topology of the network, independently from the VLANS
opzioneB: Starting from the already configured VLANS
opzioneC: Starting from the already configured VLANs, creating a different tree for every VLAN
opzioneD: Starting from the physical topology of the network, taking into account the already configured VLANS
motivazione: Standard 802.1D computes a single Common Spanning Tree (CST) across the physical topology, regardless of VLANs.

domanda: 53. We need the Spanning Tree because:
opzioneA: The learning process in bridges cannot operate when the L2 network contains loops
opzioneB: The forward learning process in bridges is not optimized in case of multiple paths toward the destination
opzioneC: In case the network contains multiple paths, the load balancing algorithm can generate frame reordering problems in the destination host
opzioneD: Frames can circulate forever since the Time-To-Live field in the Ethernet frame is not decremented by bridges
motivazione: Without STP, L2 loops cause endless frame circulation and MAC address thrashing because Ethernet has no TTL field.

domanda: 54. Given two bridges connected to the same L2 network, in which the Bridge Identifiers are 32768-00:11:22:33:44:55 and 28672-00:22:33:44:55:66:
opzioneA: The first will become the root bridge
opzioneB: The second will become the root bridge
opzioneC: The value 28672 is not a valid Bridge Priority
opzioneD: The second bridge will become root for the VLAN 0, while the first will become root bridge for all the other VLANs
motivazione: In STP elections, a lower Bridge Priority value takes precedence (28672 is less than 32768).

domanda: 55. Differently from the Spanning Tree Protocol, the Rapid Spanning Tree Protocol:
opzioneA: Supports networks that include Layer-1 hubs
opzioneB: Guarantees fast convergence time
opzioneC: Allows to use fiber optic in the network
opzioneD: Allows to use different VLANs in the network
motivazione: RSTP (802.1w) introduces immediate port state transitions, cutting convergence time from 50 seconds to a few seconds.

domanda: 56. When a switch detects that one of its links gets interrupted, the Rapid Spanning Tree Protocol deletes the corresponding entries in the forwarding database:
opzioneA: When the forward delay timer expires
opzioneB: When the max age timer expires
opzioneC: Immediately
opzioneD: It will set the aging time of those entries to (Max Age Forward Delay) and then waits for them to expire
motivazione: To ensure rapid reconvergence, RSTP flushes MAC address entries immediately upon detecting a topology change.

domanda: 57. The Rapid Spanning Tree protocol can guarantees a faster convergence if:
opzioneA: All the links are point-to-point
opzioneB: All the links operate in full-duplex mode
opzioneC: The network does not contain circular paths
opzioneD: The network is structured in a tree-like fashion
motivazione: Full-duplex point-to-point links allow RSTP to bypass listening/learning states and transition to forwarding immediately.

domanda: 58. The Multiple Spanning Tree protocol is useful because:
opzioneA: It reduces the broadcast domain of the network by dividing it into areas called Regions
opzioneB: It works in networks that include hubs
opzioneC: It guarantees faster convergence time with respect to STP and RSTP
opzioneD: It makes the network more scalable, by dividing it into areas called Regions
motivazione: MSTP scales efficiently by grouping multiple VLANs into a few spanning tree instances (Regions), reducing CPU load.

domanda: 59. According to the IEEE 802.1p standard, the priority can be associated to a packet:
opzioneA: Only by the port of the switch
opzioneB: Only by a network interface (NIthat support this type of function
opzioneC: Either by the port on the switch or by a network interface (NIthat support this function
opzioneD: Only by the application that generates the traffic
motivazione: 802.1p QoS bits can be marked either by the ingress switchport or inherently by a compatible endpoint NIC.

domanda: 60. The port of the switch connected to a station that is able to associate the "priority" to packets must be:
opzioneA: In Trunk or Access mode
opzioneB: Only in Trunk mode
opzioneC: Only in Access mode
opzioneD: One side of the cable in Trunk mode and the other one in Access
motivazione: 802.1p priority bits are carried inside the 802.1Q tag, thus requiring a trunk or tagged port configuration.

domanda: 61. The 802.1p standard defines:
opzioneA: Several classes of service for different kinds of traffic
opzioneB: Several priorities for different kinds of traffic
opzioneC: A Best-Effort class at lower priority with other ones (configurable as you want) at higher priority
opzioneD: A group of traffic classes served by a Round Robin scheduler
motivazione: 802.1p defines 8 separate Classes of Service (CoS) values to prioritize different types of Layer 2 traffic.

domanda: 62. The 802.3x standard defines:
opzioneA: A Pause packet to be used in case of host congestion
opzioneB: A Pause packet to be used in case of network congestion
opzioneC: A Pause packet to be used in case of congestion (host or switch)
opzioneD: A mechanism to assign priority to voice traffic
motivazione: 802.3x flow control allows a congested node to send PAUSE frames to halt incoming traffic and prevent buffer overflow.

domanda: 63. The Pause packet in the 802.3x standard:
opzioneA: Is sent to the transmitting host in order to reduce the amount of traffic injected in the network
opzioneB: Is sent to the transmitting host in order to stop temporarily the traffic injected in the network
opzioneC: Is sent to the device connected at the other side of the link in order to reduce the amount of traffic injected in the network
opzioneD: Can cause temporary interruptions of the network traffic
motivazione: PAUSE frames force the sender to halt transmission completely, which can cause jitter and temporary traffic interruptions.

domanda: 64. In a campus network with a 10Gbps backbone, in which we want to transport also voice traffic:
opzioneA: You do not need to configure any QoS mechanism
opzioneB: It is recommended to implement the 802.3x standard (Pause packets)
opzioneC: It is recommended to implement the 802.1p standard associating the highest priority to the voice traffic
opzioneD: It is reasonable to implement the 802.1p standard associating a portion of the transmission bandwidth to the voice traffic ("Round Robin" scheduler)
motivazione: Using 802.1p scheduling ensures latency-sensitive voice traffic is served adequately without being dropped.

domanda: 65. The IEEE 802.3ad standard (Link Aggregation) allows to:
opzioneA: Increase the bandwidth on a single point-to-point connection
opzioneB: Create a redundant point-to-point connection, without increasing the bandwidth
opzioneC: Create a redundant point-to-point connection and increase the bandwidth
opzioneD: Create a redundant point-to-point connection
motivazione: LACP bundles multiple physical links into one logical connection, increasing overall throughput and providing failover redundancy.

domanda: 66. In the IEEE 802.3ad standard (Link Aggregation):
opzioneA: Both devices at the two ends of the link must support the 802.3ad standard
opzioneB: Defines a maximum number of ports that can be part of the logic aggregation
opzioneC: Requires that at least one of the devices at the two ends of the link supports the 802.3ad standard
opzioneD: Allows the links that are part of a logical aggregation to have different speeds
motivazione: LACP is a negotiation protocol; both connected endpoints must actively support and agree to aggregate the links.

domanda: 67. The IEEE 802.3ad standard (Link Aggregation) has some limitations:
opzioneA: It exists a maximum number of ports that can be part of the logic aggregation
opzioneB: The aggregation can be used only on full duplex links
opzioneC: The aggregation can be used only on half duplex links
opzioneD: The links that are part of an aggregation must have different speeds
motivazione: Link aggregation demands that all physical members of the bundle operate strictly in full-duplex mode.

domanda: 68. The IEEE 802.3ad standard (Link Aggregation) has some limitations:
opzioneA: On the maximum number of ports that can be part of the logic aggregation
opzioneB: On the load balancing traffic criteria, that must be necessarily based on L2 information
opzioneC: On the links that are part of the aggregation, that must have the same speed
opzioneD: On the use of the Spanning Tree Protocol, that is not supported on logical aggregated links
motivazione: To function properly, all physical links grouped in an aggregate must operate at identical speeds and duplexes.

domanda: 69. A motivation for the IEEE 802.3ad standard (Link Aggregation) is:
opzioneA: Allows to create a redundant connection in addition to a existing point-to-point link
opzioneB: Allows the concurrent usage of multiple links between 2 switches even in presence of the Spanning Tree Protocol
opzioneC: Makes the update of the filtering database faster, even when the Spanning Tree Protocol is used
opzioneD: Avoids congestions in the network due to the better utilization of the available bandwidth
motivazione: LACP hides the redundancy from STP; instead of blocking extra links, STP sees them as a single logical forwarding link.

domanda: 70. In case of the Link Aggregation standard, identify which sentence is wrong:
opzioneA: The links that are grouped in the same logical aggregate must have the same speed
opzioneB: The links that are grouped in the same logical aggregate must operate in full-duplex mode
opzioneC: The links that are grouped in the same logical aggregate must be attached to the same devices (unless some virtualization technologies such as Cisco VSS are used)
opzioneD: The links that are grouped in the same logical aggregate must be connected to a switch whose ports have the same Port Priority
motivazione: While links must match speed and duplex, individual Port Priorities do not strictly have to be identical to form a bundle.

domanda: 71. The IGMP Snooping mechanism allows:
opzioneA: To detect the presence of hosts over the LAN that are member of a multicast IPv6 group
opzioneB: To detect the presence of hosts over the LAN that are member of a multicast IPv4 group
opzioneC: To detect the presence of hosts over the LAN that are member of a multicast IPv6 or IPv4 group
opzioneD: To detect the presence of hosts over the LAN that are member of a IPv4 or IPv6 network
motivazione: IGMP Snooping listens to IPv4 IGMP messages to constrain Layer 2 multicast flooding. (MLD Snooping is used for IPv6).

domanda: 72. The IGMP Snooping function:
opzioneA: Is normally implemented on all level 3 devices present on the LAN
opzioneB: Is normally implemented on level 2 devices present on the LAN
opzioneC: Is needed in order to debug the possible multicast traffic present on the LAN
opzioneD: Is occasionally used in modern devices, whereas it was important in the past
motivazione: It is a Layer 2 optimization feature running on switches to intelligently forward multicast traffic only to interested ports.

domanda: 73. In the IGMP Snooping function:
opzioneA: Router intercepts IGMP membership Report packets
opzioneB: Switch intercepts IGMP membership Report packets
opzioneC: Router intercepts both IGMP membership Query/Report packets
opzioneD: Switch intercepts IGMP membership Query/Report packets
motivazione: The switch intercepts both Queries (from routers) and Reports (from hosts) to build its multicast forwarding table.

domanda: 74. The IGMP Snooping function:
opzioneA: Affects all multicast traffic on the LAN
opzioneB: Affects all IP multicast traffic on the LAN
opzioneC: Affects a part of IP multicast traffic on the LAN
opzioneD: Affects the part of IP multicast traffic that does not make use of the IGMP protocol
motivazione: It optimizes specifically those IP multicast groups that hosts have explicitly joined via the IGMP protocol.

domanda: 75. The HSRP and VSRP protocols are used to:
opzioneA: Propagate automatically the information related to the presence of VLANs in the network
opzioneB: Assign different priorities to packets belonging to different VLANs
opzioneC: Manage automatically the configuration (trunk/access) of the switch ports according to the configuration of the network interface of the PC connected on the other side of the cable
opzioneD: Provide default gateway redundancy
motivazione: HSRP and VRRP provide a virtual IP and MAC to serve as a highly available, redundant default gateway for hosts.

domanda: 76. A LAN in which the VRRP/HSRP protocols are active:
opzioneA: ARP replies are generated by the two routers in load balancing
opzioneB: ARP replies are always generated by the router that booted first
opzioneC: ARP replies are always generated by the primary router
opzioneD: ARP replies are always generated in multi cast
motivazione: The Active (HSRP) or Master (VRRP) router temporarily owns the virtual MAC and exclusively replies to ARP requests.

domanda: 77. The track functionality of the HSRP protocol can be used to:
opzioneA: Set automatically the IP address of the HSRP virtual router
opzioneB: Set dynamically the MAC address of the HSRP virtual router
opzioneC: Influence the selection of the interface in Active state according to the status of another interface
opzioneD: Influence the selection of the interface in Active state according to the IP address configured on the router
motivazione: Tracking allows a router to decrement its HSRP priority if an external uplink fails, triggering a failover to the standby.

domanda: 78. HSRP messages are encapsulated in:
opzioneA: TCP
opzioneB: UDP
opzioneC: Directly into IP packets
opzioneD: Directly into Ethernet frames
motivazione: HSRP uses UDP port 1985 to transmit its hello messages between peers.

domanda: 79. VRRP messages are encapsulated in:
opzioneA: TCP
opzioneB: UDP
opzioneC: Directly into IP packets
opzioneD: Directly into Ethernet frames
motivazione: VRRP is encapsulated directly over IP, using IP protocol number 112.

domanda: 80. In HSRP, the two main routers in a LAN can assume the following states:
opzioneA: Master or Slave
opzioneB: Active or Standby
opzioneC: Master or Backup
opzioneD: Active or Inactive
motivazione: HSRP terminology designates the primary router as 'Active' and the backup as 'Standby'.

domanda: 81. In VRRP, the routers in a LAN can assume the following states:
opzioneA: Master or Slave
opzioneB: Active o Standby
opzioneC: Master or Backup
opzioneD: Active or Inactive
motivazione: VRRP terminology designates the primary router as 'Master' and the backup as 'Backup'.

domanda: 82. In the transmission of HSRP messages, the TTL field of the corresponding IP packet is set:
opzioneA: To a random value; when the packet arrives to the router it will be discarded automatically, thus avoiding the forwarding out of the LAN
opzioneB: To value "255" and the routers are forced to discard HSRP messages that contain a TTL different from 255
opzioneC: To value "0", thus avoiding that the packet is forwarded out of the LAN
opzioneD: To value "1", thus avoiding that the packet is forwarded out of the LAN
motivazione: A TTL of 1 ensures that HSRP multicasts stay strictly within the local broadcast domain and are not routed outward.

domanda: 83. In the transmission of VRRP messages, the TTL field of the corresponding IP packet is set:
opzioneA: To a random value; when the packet arrives to the router it will be discarded automatically, thus avoiding the forwarding out of the LAN
opzioneB: To value "255" and the routers are forced to discard HSRP messages that contain a TTL different from 255
opzioneC: To value "0", thus avoiding that the packet is forwarded out of the LAN
opzioneD: To value "1", thus avoiding that the packet is forwarded out of the LAN
motivazione: VRRP enforces a TTL of 255; if a packet arrives with a lower value, it implies remote routing (a potential spoofing attempt) and is dropped.

domanda: 84. In the HSRP protocol, the load balancing functionality on geographical links:
opzioneA: Is provided automatically
opzioneB: Is provided automatically only for the traffic exiting towards the Internet
opzioneC: Is provided automatically only for the incoming traffic in the LAN
opzioneD: Is not under the control of the HSRP protocol
motivazione: Load balancing is not inherent to basic HSRP; it requires manual configuration via Multiple HSRP (MHSRP) groups.

domanda: 85. In case of failure of a geographical link connected to an HSRP router in Active state:
opzioneA: The HSRP protocol might change the status of the interface in Active state when the function "track" is enabled
opzioneB: The HSRP protocol automatically elects the other router as Active
opzioneC: The HSRP protocol will not change the status of the routers (i.e. the current router will still be Active)
opzioneD: The HSRP protocol will send an ICMP Router Redirect message to all the hosts on the LAN in order to force them to change their default gateway and to use the second router
motivazione: If interface tracking is configured, the failed link causes the Active router to lower its priority, allowing the Standby to preempt.

domanda: 86. The phenomenon of flooding of packets on networks that have a redundant default gateway managed by HSRP:
opzioneA: Can occur when there are asymmetric paths (ingress/ egress) of traffic
opzioneB: Can happen only in case of problems on the Spanning Tree Protocol
opzioneC: Can happen only in case some broadcast frames are transmitted
opzioneD: Can never happen
motivazione: Return traffic mapped to the Standby router causes flooding because the Standby router's MAC table hasn't learned the internal endpoints.

domanda: 87. We usually define a wire-speed (or full-speeLayer-3 switch if it is able to:
opzioneA: Send packets only in full-duplex mode
opzioneB: Send packets only in half-duplex mode
opzioneC: Send packets in both full-duplex and half-duplex mode
opzioneD: Forward an amount of packets equal to the traffic received on all its network interfaces at the same time and at their full rate
motivazione: Wire-speed implies hardware ASICs can process and route packets identically to the physical maximum ingress rate of the ports.

domanda: 88. Layer 4 switches are devices with hardware support to execute operations:
opzioneA: Based on information of Layer 4 and lower layers
opzioneB: Based on information of Layer 4 and higher layers
opzioneC: Based only on Layer 4 information
opzioneD: Based only on Layers 3 and 4 information
motivazione: Layer 4 switches inspect L2/L3 headers alongside L4 data (like TCP/UDP port numbers) to make intelligent routing decisions.

domanda: 89. In the design of the core portion of a campus network, it should be better to select network devices:
opzioneA: That are able to compute quickly the routing tables
opzioneB: That are able to manage very big routing table
opzioneC: With high data forwarding capacity
opzioneD: That are able to support a high number of different data link technologies
motivazione: Core networks prioritize raw, high-speed packet switching over complex policy or large routing table management.

domanda: 90. In case of a network that uses multilayer switches (L2-L3 switches):
opzioneA: Each link that connects a host to the switch is served by an IP network with netmask/30
opzioneB: Each device (switch) has an IP address for each VLAN
opzioneC: The network manager can decide, for each interface of the switch, if it must be configured at level 2 or level 3
opzioneD: We need to use VLANs
motivazione: Multilayer switch ports are flexible; they can act as standard L2 switchports or be assigned IP addresses as L3 routed ports.

domanda: 91. In case of a network that uses multilayer switches (L2-L3 switches), with the Spanning Tree turned on:
opzioneA: We need first to determine the outcome of Spanning Tree, and then which are the HSRP active/standby routers
opzioneB: We need first to determine which are the HSRP active/standby routers, and then the outcome of Spanning Tree
opzioneC: The HSRP active/standby routers and outcome of Spanning Tree are independent issues, although we should determine both
opzioneD: We need to determine the outcome of Spanning Tree
motivazione: STP dictates the baseline L2 physical topology, which must be resolved before L3 protocols like HSRP can operate reliably over it.

domanda: 92. In case of a network that uses multilayer switches (L2-3 switches):
opzioneA: Paths are more optimized if the root bridge overlaps with the possible active router deter- mined by HSRP/VRRP
opzioneB: Paths are more optimized if the root bridge does not overlap with the possible active router determined by HSRP/VRRP
opzioneC: The path determined by the Spanning Tree and the possible active router determined by HSRP/VRRP are indipendent concepts and therefore they do not affect how traffic is propogated over the network
opzioneD: Paths mainly depend on the configuration of level 3 addresses associated with VLANs
motivazione: Aligning the STP Root Bridge with the HSRP Active router prevents traffic from crossing suboptimal L2 blocked paths before exiting the subnet.

domanda: 93. In a Content Delivery Network, the content of a server (e.g. web pages) are replicated and:
opzioneA: Distributed over the network, provided that all servers are physically close together
opzioneB: Distributed over the network, provided that all servers are physically far each other
opzioneC: Distributed over the network, no matter of the physical location of servers
opzioneD: Distributed over the network, provided that all servers belong to the same IP subnet
motivazione: CDNs distribute content logically based on global presence and user proximity, decoupled from single physical locations.

domanda: 94. In a DNS-based Content Delivery Network:
opzioneA: The DNS answers to users' HTTP requests redirecting them to the nearest cache
opzioneB: The DNS answers to domain names requests (e.g "cnn.com") redirecting them to the nearest DNS cache
opzioneC: The DNS returns an IP address according to the requested DNS name (e.g. "[www.cnn.com](https://www.cnn.com)") and the source IP address of the client
opzioneD: The DNS returns an IP address according to the requested DNSname (e.g. "[www.cnn.com](https://www.cnn.com)") and the destination IP address of the client
motivazione: DNS-based CDNs use the requesting client's DNS resolver IP to intelligently return the IP address of the geographically closest edge cache.

domanda: 95. A Load Balancing Server can operate according to the following principles:
opzioneA: Content-unaware (layer 4 switching) and Content-aware (layer 7 switching)
opzioneB: Content-unaware (layer 7 switching) and Content-aware (layer 4 switching)
opzioneC: Content-unaware (layer 4 switching) and DNS-based Routing
opzioneD: DNS-based Routing and Content-aware (layer 7 switching)
motivazione: Load balancers generally act at Layer 4 (IP/Ports without inspecting payload) or Layer 7 (inspecting application logic like HTTP headers).

domanda: 96. A Content-unaware Load Balancing Server is able to distribute the load according to:
opzioneA: Information present in layer 3, 4 protocols and to the DNS name requested
opzioneB: Information present in layer 3 and 4
opzioneC: Information present in layer 4 and 7
opzioneD: Information present in layer 2, 3, 4 and 7
motivazione: Content-unaware balancers make forwarding decisions solely using Layer 3 (IP addresses) and Layer 4 (TCP/UDP ports) headers.

domanda: 97. A Sticky Connection:
opzioneA: Identifies an HTTP connection whose state must be maintained for the entire duration of connection
opzioneB: Identify the set of HTTP connections that share some common state (e.g. shopping chart)
opzioneC: Requires that the Server Load Balancer forwards the traffic of a user always to the same physical server
opzioneD: Requires that the web server involved in the transaction stores user data on the same disk of the Storage Area Network
motivazione: Sticky sessions map a specific user's sequence of related HTTP requests to the exact same physical backend server to maintain application state.

domanda: 98. In the event that a user connects to a web service that is powered by a battery of physical servers behind a Load Balancer Server, the use of cookies within HTTP sessions:
opzioneA: It may be necessary to handle sticky connections
opzioneB: It may be necessary to store the credentials of a user
opzioneC: It is a technique commonly used to violate privacy of the user
opzioneD: It is a technique to create persistent pop-ups on the user desktop
motivazione: HTTP cookies are the primary method L7 load balancers use to identify a user session and route them to their sticky backend server.

domanda: 99. Which one among the following points does not apply to Network Attached Storage (NAS):
opzioneA: Most operating systems are able to mount a shared disk without additional drivers
opzioneB: The NAS is able to operate on Wide Area Networks
opzioneC: Minimum impact on existing infrastructure
opzioneD: The client have full control over the disc
motivazione: NAS provides file-level access over a network; the client OS does not have raw block-level control over the physical disk.

domanda: 100. A Network Attached Storage (NAS):
opzioneA: It is typically used to allow the client to boot from the network
opzioneB: It is typically used to share the disks on Wide Area Networks
opzioneC: Allows clients to have full control over the disc
opzioneD: It has a minimal impact on network infrastructure and existing software
motivazione: NAS uses standard network protocols (like SMB or NFS), integrating easily into existing IP networks without heavy infrastructure changes.

domanda: 101. In Storage Area Networks (SAN), the virtualization of the storage space is implemented at:
opzioneA: File system level
opzioneB: Physical level (blocks on the disk)
opzioneC: At choice, File System or Physical level (blocks on the disk)
opzioneD: Physical level (blocks on the disk) as long as you use a set of specific File Systems
motivazione: Unlike NAS, SANs provide raw block-level access to storage, requiring the client OS to format and manage the file system.

domanda: 102. A possible protocol stack used in a Storage Area Network (SAN) is:
opzioneA: Fiber Channel - Ethernet
opzioneB: Fiber Channel IP Ethernet
opzioneC: iSCSI UDP IP Ethernet
opzioneD: CIFSTCP-IP Ethernet
motivazione: Fibre Channel over Ethernet (FCoE) encapsulates raw Fibre Channel frames directly over Ethernet for high-speed block storage.

domanda: 103. The IEEE 802.1x standard describes the behavior of the EAPOL authentication protocol, which is used to allow the communication between:
opzioneA: Supplicant and Authentication server
opzioneB: Authenticator and Authentication server
opzioneC: Supplicant and Authenticator
opzioneD: Supplicant and Authenticator, and then between Authenticator and Authentication server
motivazione: EAPOL (EAP over LAN) defines the L2 communication strictly between the client (Supplicant) and the access switch (Authenticator).

domanda: 104. The EAPOL protocol defined in the standard IEEE 802.1x is a:
opzioneA: Layer 2 protocol
opzioneB: Layer 3 protocol
opzioneC: Layer 4 protocol
opzioneD: Layer 7 protocol
motivazione: EAPOL operates strictly at Layer 2 to control physical port access before any IP address (Layer 3) is even assigned.

domanda: 105. Inside the IEEE 802.1x specification, the RADIUS protocol:
opzioneA: Defines the necessary data to the authentication of the user
opzioneB: Transports the data concerning the authentication of the user to the RADIUS authentication server
opzioneC: Allows the RADIUS authentication server to communicate with the server that keeps the database of the users (es. LDAP, Active Directory, etc.) in the selected domain
opzioneD: Allows the exchange of appropriate authentication messages between the client (supplicant) and the access Server (Authenticator)
motivazione: The switch (Authenticator) repackages the client's EAP credentials into RADIUS packets to securely communicate with the backend Authentication Server.

domanda: 106. The dynamic associations of a port on a switch to a VLAN that depends on the user that is currently connecting over that port:
opzioneA: Cannot be done
opzioneB: Is the default solution in VLAN configuration
opzioneC: Can be done through a particular extension of the protocol 802.1x
opzioneD: Can be done only if the user station has the port configured as "trunk"
motivazione: 802.1x allows the RADIUS server to push dynamic VLAN assignments down to the switch port upon successful user authentication.

domanda: 107. The protocol 802.1x recognizes the user according to:
opzioneA: Proper credentials (e.g. Username/password)
opzioneB: The MAC address of the Network Interface Card (NIof the user
opzioneC: The IP address configured on the interface of the host
opzioneD: The DNS name associated to the user
motivazione: 802.1x relies on explicit identity credentials (like EAP-PEAP usernames or EAP-TLS certificates), providing much stronger security than easily spoofed MAC addresses.
